CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Continued To Jan-10-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The Plaintiff's 2nd Amended Complaint is a duplicate of the 1st Amended Complaint but with the addition of two new common counts, one which merely alleges that the human body is a magnet and the human brain is a magnetic field susceptible to magnetic signals. The second new common count alleges that Plaintiff's mental and physical problems "have told him" that he is controlled by magnetic signals. The plaintiff has failed to allege facts that entitle him to the requested relief against Defendant City and County of San Francisco for Assault and Battery or for a violation of federal Civil Rights. Plaintiff has failed to allege who in the SFPD engaged in what specific conduct that constitutes an assault or a battery. Plaintiff has failed to allege where and how radio signals were set up and directed at only him. Plaintiff has failed to allege facts of how he knew the assault and/or battery was occurring. A trial court does not abuse its discretion by sustaining a demurrer without leave to amend if it appears from the complaint that there is no reasonable possibility that an amendment could cure the defect. Blickman Turkus, LP vs MF Downtown Sunnyvale, LLC (2008) 162 Cal.App.4th 858, 867. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Gail Dekreon; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-03-2017 Continued To Nov-15-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant The City And County Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SUSAN J. KAWALA, DEPUTY CITY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-3827 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to plaintiff Sui Hong Yee's first amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. The first amended complaint is uncertain as to the circumstances of the alleged mind control by radio signals of the San Francisco Police Department including who in SFPD engaged in what specific conduct, how they performed the mind control alleged, and how Mr. Yee knows of the mind control. Mr. Yee is granted twenty days leave to amend to cure these deficiencies. The City has not submitted authority binding on this court that would warrant sustaining the demurrer without leave to amend. Best v. Kelly (D.C. Cir. 1994) 39 F.3d 328, 330-331 and Curran v. Holder (2009) 626 F.Supp.2d 30 deal with federal subject matter jurisdiction which is not relevant to this case. Under California law, a demurrer admits "all material facts properly pleaded." (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) This is true no matter how improbable the facts may be. (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.) The third "cause of action" for injunctive relief is not a separate cause of action but a remedy. Mr. Yee is permitted to request injunctive relief in his second amended complaint as such. Finally, the City has not shown that Mr. Yee's claims are barred by the applicable statute of limitations. Plaintiff has alleged that "[h]e has been controlled by the San Francisco Police Department for over twenty three years through such electromagnetic waves and radio signals and such control is still on going." [First Amended Complaint, par. 9] [emphasis added]. Since plaintiff has alleged ongoing conduct, the statute of limitations did not run before plaintiff filed his government tort claim or his complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge: Stephen Murphy; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/sm
